title: Sri Lanka secures IMF staff-level deal for $700m as markets rally
body: IMF staff and the authorities reached staff-level agreement on the combined Fifth and Sixth Reviews of the Extended Fund Facility, paving the way for about $700 million and taking total potential disbursements to roughly $2.4 billion upon Board approval. Equity investors cheered calmer Middle East risk and the prospect of IMF funds: on 8 April the ASPI jumped 4.21% and the S&P SL20 4.42%, while turnover swelled to Rs. 6.61 billion. 💰

title: Parliament scrutinises Rs. 13.2bn NDB fraud; regulator freezes dividends and orders forensic audit
body: Lawmakers debated a National Development Bank PLC fraud that escalated from an initial Rs. 380 million to Rs. 13.2 billion within days, prompting questions on supervisory gaps. Regulators imposed a cash-dividend suspension, curbs on discretionary spending and branch expansion, and directed the appointment of an independent forensic auditor, while criminal investigations proceed.

title: Public transport modernisation advances with digital ticketing, Fort hub revamp and Northern Line repair
body: Commercial Bank of Ceylon and NCG Express launched fully digital bus ticketing accepting cards and QR payments, integrating with the bank’s acquiring network for real‑time settlement. The Government unveiled a Rs. 2.16 billion, ADB‑backed upgrade of Colombo Fort station—automated ticketing, lifts and safer access—targeted for completion in 15 months; India-financed works restored cyclone‑damaged sections of the Northern Railway under a $5 million grant.

title: Solar build‑out gathers pace as coal quality lapses trigger health and cost concerns
body: The International Solar Alliance advanced a country partnership strategy in Colombo to mobilise investment for a 4 GW pipeline, alongside corporate installations such as Hayleys' 800 kW rooftop for LSEG in Malabe. Separately, audit and emissions data around the Norochcholai plant reported coal shipments with ash content above 21%—nearly double stated norms—and a Public Utilities Commission estimate of fly‑ash discharge roughly doubling to 0.093 kg/kWh, heightening environmental and operational risks. ⚡

title: State machinery moves on overdue polls and digital identity amid graft case
body: A Parliamentary Select Committee examined legal routes to break the deadlock over Provincial Council elections, including repealing the 2017 mixed system to revert to the prior framework, and sought opinions from the Attorney General and Delimitation Commission. The Cabinet appointed H. M. J. M. Herath as Commissioner General of Registration of Persons to accelerate the Sri Lanka Digital Identity Card, while the Motor Traffic Commissioner General was further remanded to 20 April in a vehicle‑registration fraud case.
